steps.title: Turn the charges into points
steps.content: The first two hours cost 220 dollars, so the total-cost line passes through $(2,220)$. Five hours cost 400 dollars, so it also passes through $(5,400)$. The slope is $$\frac{400-220}{5-2}=60,$$ meaning each extra hour costs 60 dollars.

steps.title: Read the fitted line
steps.content: Use $f(x)=60x+b$ with $(2,220)$: $$220=60(2)+b$$ $$b=100$$ Therefore $$f(x)=60x+100$$ This is faster than expanding the base-fee expression by hand.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: A is $f(x)=60x+100$, and it satisfies both $f(2)=220$ and $f(5)=400$. Another option has the right value at $x=5$? No: ${80}(5)=400$, but it gives $f(2)=160$, not 220. A is correct.
